pattern: Greedy + DP: deduplicate and sort reward values, then use a 1-D boolean DP over reachable totals — after sorting, the optimal strategy always picks values in increasing order.
hints: Sort and deduplicate the reward values. If you pick values in increasing order, each newly picked value v must exceed your current total x, meaning x < v, so x can be at most v-1.
Let dp[x] = whether total x is achievable. After considering all values <= v, a new value v can be added to any reachable total x where x < v, giving dp[x + v] = true.
The answer is the largest reachable total. The DP table size is bounded by 2*max(rewardValues), so for this variant (values <= 2000) a simple bitmask or boolean array suffices.

edge_inputs.description: all identical values — can only take one
value 1 appears many times, contributes only once

stress.description: random array of length up to 200 with values up to 200
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    vals = [rng.randint(1, size) for _ in range(n)]
    return [vals]
